web_results.title: Iran agrees to end enrichment of uranium by March 31? Pr... | Polymarket
web_results.description: Propose resolutionThis market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran publicly agrees to end all enrichment of uranium by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

web_results.title: Iran's Nuclear Timetable: The Weapon Potential | Iran Watch
web_results.description: How quickly could Iran get enough fissile material for a small nuclear arsenal? This timetable estimates how quickly Iran could amass enough weapons-grade uranium for at least five bombs. Once it has the enriched uranium, however, it could take at least several months to turn it into a working ...

web_results.title: What Is Iran's nuclear program and what happened in US deal talks? - Newsweek
web_results.description: Tehran and Washington had restarted Omani-brokered talks in February with President Donald Trump setting an early March deadline for an agreement to be reached. The U.S. was demanding Iran dismantle its main nuclear facilities and transfer its ...

web_results.title: Iran agrees in breakthrough talks not to stockpile enriched uranium that could build bombs
web_results.description: IRAN HAS AGREED never to stockpile enriched uranium, mediator Oman’s top diplomat says, in what he called a breakthrough that he hoped would prevent war.

web_results.title: Nuclear program of Iran - Wikipedia
web_results.description: Iran and the US have engaged in bilateral negotiations since April 2025, aiming to curb Iran's program for sanctions relief, though Iran's leaders have refused to stop enriching uranium.

news_results.title: UN nuclear watchdog says it's unable to verify whether Iran has suspended all uranium enrichment | PBS News
news_results.description: The agency says Iran has four declared enrichment facilities, but it has lost "continuity of knowledge" about stockpiles.

news_results.title: Trump wonders why Iran won’t ‘capitulate’ on its nuclear program. Here’s why | CNN
news_results.description: “Curious to know why we do not capitulate? Because we are IRANIAN,” Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi wrote after US envoy Steve Witkoff said President Donald Trump was wondering why Tehran hadn’t yielded to American pressure over its nuclear program.

news_results.title: As risk of war looms, U.S.-Iran nuclear talks wrap with no deal announced | PBS News
news_results.description: Trump wants a deal to constrain Iran’s nuclear program, and he sees an opportunity while the country is struggling at home with growing dissent following nationwide protests.

news_results.title: Iran Update, February 27, 2026 | ISW
news_results.description: The IAEA cannot verify whether Iran has suspended “all enrichment-related activities,” according to a confidential report seen by the AP.

news_results.title: Peace ‘within reach’ as Iran agrees no nuclear material stockpile: Oman FM | Military News | Al Jazeera
news_results.description: Oman's Foreign Minister says most recent indirect talks between US, Iran 'really advanced, substantially'.
